squads.name: Omnis
squads.description: Router squad (default for new chats): hands each request to the squad best suited to handle it. Leaderless — the Omnis agent runs directly and only routes.

squads.name: Default
squads.description: General-purpose coordinator for coding and local-system tasks. Web research lives in the Knowledge squad and Kubernetes work in the Kubernetes squad.

squads.name: Kubernetes
squads.description: Kubernetes management & triage specialist. Diagnoses unhealthy workloads (crash-looping pods, deployments not ready, services unreachable) and investigates pod logs via read-only kubectl. Route here for anything mentioning kubernetes, k8s, kubectl, pods, deployments, replicasets, services, namespaces, nodes, helm, or a kubectl error.

squads.name: Knowledge
squads.description: Information retrieval & document creation specialist. Web research and fact-finding, extracting text from PDFs/DOCX/PPTX/XLSX/images (including OCR), and converting or generating documents across formats (Markdown, DOCX, PDF, HTML, LaTeX). Route here for research/'who is'/'what is'/'find or summarise information about…' questions, including whether some software/library/tool exists out in the world (e.g. 'is there a transparent HTTP proxy in Rust?'), document parsing/extraction, OCR, and format conversion or generation.

squads.name: Skill Editor
squads.description: Skill editing focus: Skill editor plus the web agent for research and reference retrieval, and the Helper for sourcing existing skills and answering questions about omnis.

squads.name: Helper
squads.description: Answers questions about omnis itself and its capabilities, finds/inspects/installs omnis registry items (skills, agents, MCP servers, squads, A2A peers, commands), AND reads or changes any omnis SETTING on request. Use this when the user asks whether OMNIS has an agent/skill/tool/MCP server for a task (even a specialised one like Flux CD or Kubernetes), wants to discover or install one, asks what omnis can do or how it works, OR wants to view or change a setting/configuration — e.g. 'change the theme', 'switch the coder agent to model X', 'set my language to French', 'allow kubectl get', 'add an MCP server', 'edit a permission/hook', 'which model does agent Y use', OR wants to UNDO/REVERT a settings change ('revert that', 'undo your last change', 'go back to the initial state') — i.e. omnis (or 'you') is the subject. NOT for general world-knowledge or research questions about whether some software/library exists (e.g. 'is there an HTTP proxy library in Rust?') — those go to the Knowledge squad. Leaderless: the Helper agent runs directly, limited to its own docs + registries + settings tools.

squads.name: Coding
squads.description: Software-engineering specialist for substantial coding work on real projects: navigating and editing large codebases with language-server intelligence (go-to-definition, find-references, hover types, project-wide diagnostics and safe rename), planning multi-step changes, isolating risky work in git worktrees, and verifying every edit by re-checking diagnostics and running builds/tests in the background. Route here for implementing features, refactoring, fixing bugs, or any non-trivial change to a codebase (as opposed to one-off scripts). Leaderless: the coder agent runs directly.
